content.full_text:
CaliforrJ~ 
Fair Political 
Practices Commission 
Leanne Chipchase 
P.o. Box 11485 
santa Rosa, CA 
95402 
Dear Ms. Chipchase: 
october 18, 1985 
This letter will confirm our telephone conversation of 
October 17, 1985. 
Retired judges on assignment in courts of 
record are required to file statements of Economic Interests 
only if they serve or expect to serve more than 30 days in any 
12 month period. 
(See newly adopted FPPC Regulation 18724 
enclosed. ) 
If you have any further questions, please feel free to 
call me at (916) 322-5662. 
AH:bb 
Enclosures 
Sincerely, 
Alice Hughes 
Documents Examiner 
428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento CA 95804~0807 • (916) 322~5660

sections.conclusion: Retired judges on assignment in courts of record are required to file statements of Economic Interests only if they serve or expect to serve more than 30 days in any 12 month period.
sections.question_synthetic: Whether retired judges serving on assignment in courts of record are required to file statements of Economic Interests, and if so, under what conditions.
sections.conclusion_synthetic: Retired judges on assignment in courts of record must file statements of Economic Interests only if they serve or are expected to serve more than 30 days in any 12-month period, pursuant to FPPC Regulation 18724.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Advice letter confirming telephone conversation; no formal QUESTION or CONCLUSION section headers but substantive Q&A content present; document references newly adopted regulation 18724 provided as enclosure.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: Whether retired judges serving on assignment in courts of record are required to file statements of Economic Interests, and if so, under what conditions.

CONCLUSION: Retired judges on assignment in courts of record are required to file statements of Economic Interests only if they serve or expect to serve more than 30 days in any 12 month period.
